wordpress.txt:
*   WordPress is a system for managing website content. It began for blogs but now builds many types of websites.
*   It is free, open-source software built using the PHP programming language. WordPress needs to be installed on a web server to function.
*   Themes let users change a website's appearance. They also modify how the site works without altering its core content.
*   Plugins add new features or change existing ones. Thousands of plugins are available to tailor a site to specific needs.
*   WordPress was created in 2003 by two developers. It is owned by the WordPress Foundation.
OSM.txt:
*   OpenStreetMap (OSM) is a map database. Volunteers maintain it through open collaboration.
*   Steve Coast founded OpenStreetMap in 2004. He wanted map data to be freely accessible.
*   Volunteers collect map data from surveys. They also trace from aerial images.
*   The OpenStreetMap Foundation hosts the database. This non-profit organization relies on donations.
*   OSM stores map features in a special way. It uses points, lines, and relationships.
wayback_machine.txt:
*   The Wayback Machine is a digital archive of the World Wide Web. It lets people see how websites looked at different times in the past.
*   It was created by the Internet Archive, a nonprofit organization. Founders Brewster Kahle and Bruce Gilliat launched it publicly in 2001.
*   The archive started saving web pages as early as 1995. It now holds over a trillion web pages and vast amounts of data.
*   Users can save pages manually to the archive. New features like "Save Page Now" and an updated interface have been added.
*   Some news organizations have recently blocked the Wayback Machine. This is due to concerns about AI using the archived content.
wikipedia.txt:
*   Wikipedia is a free online encyclopedia. Volunteers write and maintain its content.
*   It was founded in 2001 by Jimmy Wales and Larry Sanger. A nonprofit organization hosts it and relies on donations.
*   Wikipedia is available in over 340 languages. It is one of the world's most visited websites.
*   It has been praised for sharing knowledge widely. Its reliability has improved over time.
*   The number of editors and page views saw some decline. However, it remains a highly popular website.
chatoyancy.txt:
*   Chatoyancy is a special light effect seen in some gemstones. It makes a bright band of light appear to move across the stone.
*   This effect is also known as the "cat's eye effect." It is caused by tiny fibers or inclusions inside the material.
*   To show chatoyancy best, gemstones are cut into a smooth, rounded shape. This cut helps the light band to be clearly visible.
*   Many gemstones, like tiger's eye, show this effect. It can also be found in certain types of wood.
*   When only "cat's eye" is said, it usually means a specific gem. This special light effect is highly valued in wood.
youtube.txt:
*   YouTube is an online video sharing platform founded in 2005 by three former PayPal employees. Google bought it in 2006, and it is now the second most visited website globally.
*   Billions of people use YouTube every month, watching over a billion hours of video daily. Hundreds of hours of new video content are uploaded every minute.
*   Google expanded YouTube's business model to include paid content and subscriptions. The platform earns billions of dollars each year from ads and subscriptions.
*   YouTube hosts many types of videos, including music, news, and vlogs. It has greatly influenced popular culture and created many new celebrities.
*   The platform has faced criticism for spreading misinformation and copyrighted content. Concerns also include user privacy and child safety issues.
8086.txt:
*   The Intel 8086 was a 16-bit computer chip. It was released by Intel on June 8, 1978.
*   This chip created the x86 architecture. This became Intel's most successful line of processors.
*   The 8086 built on earlier 8-bit Intel chips. This allowed older programs to be easily updated.
*   Its development began in 1976. New features were added to help with programming and software.
*   The chip used about 20,000 transistors. It took just over two years to develop, which was fast then.
c_lang.txt:
*   C is a programming language created in the 1970s by Dennis Ritchie. It was developed at Bell Labs.
*   It gives programmers direct control over computer hardware. C is used for operating systems and device drivers.
*   C became very popular in the 1980s. It is still one of the most widely used languages.
*   The language was designed for low-level memory access. It helps create programs that work on many computer types.
*   C has been standardized by major organizations since 1989. This ensures its consistent use across different systems.
annas_arch.txt:
*   Anna's Archive is a search engine for digital libraries. It started after another similar site faced legal action.
*   The site collects information from many digital sources. It links to files but does not host them itself.
*   It aims to catalog all books in existence. It currently holds millions of books and papers.
*   The site faces legal challenges and government blocks. This is due to claims of copyright infringement.
*   Paid memberships offer faster downloads. It also provides data for training AI models.
